subtasks.title: Write integration tests for successful event ingestion
details: Create integration test file (e.g. internal/integration/events_test.go) using Go testing package
Implement TS-01-1: POST /v1/events with valid bearer token, Content-Type: application/json, non-empty body → assert 201 Created, empty body, no Content-Type header, one row in events table
Implement TS-01-2: POST /v1/events with arbitrary JSON structure → assert 201 and raw payload stored verbatim
Implement TS-01-3: POST /v1/events → assert id is valid UUID v4 and received_at is within a few seconds of request time in UTC
Implement TS-01-4 and TS-01-5: verify token extraction format 'Bearer <token>' and that matching token allows storage

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for authentication rejection
details: Implement TS-01-E5: POST /v1/events with missing Authorization header → assert 401, Echo error body, zero rows in events table
Implement TS-01-E6: POST /v1/events with malformed Authorization header (wrong casing, extra whitespace, no 'Bearer ' prefix, all-caps) → assert 401 for each variant
Implement TS-01-E7: POST /v1/events with well-formed header but wrong token value → assert 401 and zero rows in events table

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for payload validation rejection
details: Implement TS-01-E1: POST /v1/events with valid token but missing Content-Type header → assert 400, Echo error body with 'message' key, zero rows in events table
Implement TS-01-E2: POST /v1/events with Content-Type: text/plain → assert 400, Echo error body, zero rows in events table
Implement TS-01-E3: POST /v1/events with valid token and Content-Type but empty body → assert 400, Echo error body, zero rows in events table
Implement TS-01-E4: simulate database insert failure → assert 500 Internal Server Error with Echo error body

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for health check endpoints
details: Implement TS-01-6: GET /healthz → assert 200 OK
Implement TS-01-7: GET /readyz with database available → assert 200 OK
Implement TS-01-E8: GET /readyz with database forced unavailable → assert 503 with body {"message": "service unavailable"} and Content-Type: application/json

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for configuration and startup behaviour
details: Implement TS-01-8: start service with only AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN set; assert it listens on port 8080, uses ./data/events.db, and log level defaults to info
Implement TS-01-9: start service without AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN → assert non-zero exit code and descriptive error referencing AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN, no port binding
Implement TS-01-10: start service with LOG_LEVEL=verbose → assert non-zero exit code and descriptive error referencing LOG_LEVEL

subtasks.title: Write property-based and logging tests
details: Implement TS-01-P1: for arbitrary non-empty JSON bodies with valid auth, assert exactly one row inserted, payload matches, id is UUID, received_at is UTC
Implement TS-01-P2: for all invalid auth header variants, assert 401 and no rows inserted
Implement TS-01-P3: for valid token but invalid payload variants, assert 400 and no rows inserted
Implement TS-01-P4: alternate database available/unavailable states and assert GET /readyz responds 200 or 503 accordingly
Implement TS-01-P5: for startup without AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN with various other env combos, assert non-zero exit and no port binding
Implement TS-01-11: capture log output during a request and assert each line is a valid JSON object
Implement TS-01-12: start service with LOG_LEVEL=warn and assert no debug/info entries are logged

checks: All test files compile without errors: go build ./...
Test suite runs (tests fail with 'not implemented' or similar, but no compilation errors): go test ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -run TestPlaceholder
No linter warnings introduced: golangci-lint run (or go vet ./...)
Test IDs TS-01-1 through TS-01-18, TS-01-E1 through TS-01-E8, TS-01-P1 through TS-01-P5 are all represented as named test functions in the test files

task_groups.title: Initialise Go Module and Project Layout

subtasks.title: Create Go module with correct module path
details: Run: go mod init github.com/agent-fox/example-service
Confirm go.mod contains module path github.com/agent-fox/example-service
Set Go version to 1.22 or later in go.mod

subtasks.title: Establish standard project directory layout
details: Create cmd/server/ directory for the main entrypoint (main.go)
Create internal/ directory for packages not intended for external import
Create internal/config/, internal/db/, internal/handler/, internal/middleware/ subdirectories
Add .gitignore for Go binaries and the SQLite data directory

subtasks.title: Add required third-party dependencies
details: Add Echo Framework: go get github.com/labstack/echo/v4
Add modernc.org/sqlite pure-Go SQLite driver: go get modernc.org/sqlite
Add a UUID generation library (e.g. github.com/google/uuid): go get github.com/google/uuid
Run go mod tidy to clean up go.sum
Confirm CGO_ENABLED=0 builds succeed with the selected sqlite driver

checks: go.mod contains module github.com/agent-fox/example-service
go build ./... succeeds with CGO_ENABLED=0
Directory structure matches cmd/, internal/ layout
go mod tidy produces no changes (go.sum is consistent)

subtasks.title: Define Config struct and environment variable loading
details: Create internal/config/config.go
Define Config struct with fields: Port (string, default '8080'), DBPath (string, default './data/events.db'), AuthBearerToken (string, required), LogLevel (string, default 'info')
Implement Load() function that reads PORT, DB_PATH, AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN, LOG_LEVEL from os.Getenv
Apply defaults for PORT, DB_PATH, LOG_LEVEL when env vars are absent

subtasks.title: Enforce required AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN and validate LOG_LEVEL
details: In Load(), if AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN is empty string, return a descriptive error: 'AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN environment variable is required'
Validate LOG_LEVEL against the allowed set {debug, info, warn, error}; return descriptive error if invalid: 'LOG_LEVEL must be one of: debug, info, warn, error'
Ensure Load() returns (Config, error) so callers can check errors before starting any servers

subtasks.title: Wire config loading into main entrypoint with fail-fast startup
details: In cmd/server/main.go, call config.Load() as the very first operation
On error, write descriptive message to stderr and os.Exit(1) before any port binding or database initialisation
Pass validated Config to all downstream initialisation functions

checks: TS-01-8 passes: service starts with only AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN set and defaults are applied correctly
TS-01-9 passes: service exits non-zero with AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN error message when variable is absent
TS-01-10 passes: service exits non-zero with LOG_LEVEL error message when set to 'verbose'
go vet ./internal/config/... reports no issues
01-REQ-4.2 acceptance criteria met: non-zero exit before port binding when AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN is missing

subtasks.title: Configure structured JSON logger
details: Create internal/logger/logger.go
Use a structured logging library compatible with JSON output (e.g. log/slog from Go 1.21+ with JSONHandler, or zerolog)
Implement New(level string) (*slog.Logger, error) that creates a logger with JSONHandler writing to os.Stdout
Map LOG_LEVEL string values 'debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error' to the corresponding slog.Level values

subtasks.title: Integrate logger into Echo Framework middleware
details: Configure Echo's logger middleware to use the structured JSON logger instead of default format
Ensure all HTTP request log entries are emitted as JSON objects with at minimum: time, level, method, path, status, latency fields
Pass logger instance to handler constructors so application-level log calls also emit JSON

checks: TS-01-11 passes: every log line captured during a GET /healthz request is a valid JSON object (parseable as map[string]any)
TS-01-12 passes: with LOG_LEVEL=warn, no log entries with level 'debug' or 'info' appear in output
go vet ./internal/logger/... reports no issues
01-REQ-5.1 and 01-REQ-5.2 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement SQLite database open and schema migration
details: Create internal/db/db.go
Use modernc.org/sqlite driver: sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath)
On Open, execute CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload TEXT NOT NULL, received_at DATETIME NOT NULL)
Implement Open(dbPath string) (*sql.DB, error) returning the initialised *sql.DB
Ensure the directory for DB_PATH is created if it does not exist (os.MkdirAll)

subtasks.title: Implement InsertEvent repository function
details: Create internal/db/events.go
Implement InsertEvent(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, payload string) error
Generate UUID v4 for id using github.com/google/uuid
Set received_at to time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano)
Execute INSERT INTO events (id, payload, received_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)
Return any database error to the caller without wrapping it in a generic message (let the handler decide the HTTP status)

subtasks.title: Implement database ping function for readiness probe
details: Create internal/db/ping.go
Implement Ping(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error that executes SELECT 1 and returns any error
Used by the readiness handler to determine database availability

checks: TS-01-3 passes: inserted row has valid UUID id and received_at within expected UTC time window
TS-01-2 passes: payload is stored verbatim without modification
TS-01-7 passes: Ping returns nil when database is available
go vet ./internal/db/... reports no issues
Database schema matches specification: events table with id TEXT PK, payload TEXT NOT NULL, received_at DATETIME NOT NULL

subtasks.title: Implement bearer token extraction and validation middleware
details: Create internal/middleware/auth.go
Implement BearerAuth(token string) echo.MiddlewareFunc
Extract Authorization header; if absent, return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, '...')
Check that header value begins with exactly 'Bearer ' (capital B, single space); reject with 401 if not (covers wrong casing, extra whitespace, missing prefix)
Extract the token substring after 'Bearer '; compare with constant-time strings.EqualFold-safe comparison (use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare or strings equality) against the configured token
If token does not match, return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, '...')
On success, call next handler

subtasks.title: Verify auth middleware returns no database writes on rejection
details: Confirm middleware short-circuits before any handler logic that touches the database
Auth middleware must be registered before the events handler in the Echo route chain
Add route-level middleware application: e.POST('/v1/events', handler, middleware.BearerAuth(cfg.AuthBearerToken))

checks: TS-01-E5 passes: missing Authorization header → 401
TS-01-E6 passes: all four malformed header variants → 401, zero rows in events table
TS-01-E7 passes: wrong token value → 401, zero rows in events table
TS-01-4 passes: correct 'Bearer <token>' format → 201
TS-01-P2 passes: property test for all invalid auth variants
go vet ./internal/middleware/... reports no issues
01-REQ-2.E1, 01-REQ-2.E2, 01-REQ-2.E3 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement payload validation in events handler
details: Create internal/handler/events.go
Implement EventsHandler struct with db *sql.DB field
In the handler method, check c.Request().Header.Get('Content-Type'); if not 'application/json', return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusBadRequest, '...')
Read request body; if empty (len == 0), return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusBadRequest, '...')
Do not perform any JSON schema validation beyond non-empty body check

subtasks.title: Implement event storage and 201 Created response
details: After validation passes, call db.InsertEvent(ctx, db, string(body))
On database error, return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusInternalServerError, '...')
On success, return c.NoContent(http.StatusCreated) — this sets status 201 with empty body and no Content-Type header
Confirm no Content-Type header is set on the 201 response

subtasks.title: Register events route with auth middleware in Echo router
details: In main or router setup, register: e.POST('/v1/events', eventsHandler.Handle, middleware.BearerAuth(cfg.AuthBearerToken))
Confirm route is at exactly /v1/events
Verify Echo's default error handler formats all error responses as {"message": "..."} with Content-Type: application/json

checks: TS-01-1 passes: valid request → 201 Created, empty body, no Content-Type, one row in events table
TS-01-E1 passes: missing Content-Type → 400 with {"message": ...}
TS-01-E2 passes: non-JSON Content-Type → 400 with {"message": ...}
TS-01-E3 passes: empty body → 400 with {"message": ...}
TS-01-E4 passes: DB failure → 500 with {"message": ...}
TS-01-P3 passes: property test for all invalid payload variants
01-PROP-1 invariant holds: exactly one row inserted per successful request
01-PROP-3 invariant holds: no rows inserted when payload is invalid

subtasks.title: Implement GET /healthz liveness handler
details: Create internal/handler/health.go
Implement LivenessHandler: returns c.NoContent(http.StatusOK) immediately with no database interaction
Register route: e.GET('/healthz', livenessHandler.Handle)

subtasks.title: Implement GET /readyz readiness handler
details: Implement ReadinessHandler struct with db *sql.DB field
Call db.Ping(ctx, db) inside the handler
If Ping succeeds, return c.NoContent(http.StatusOK)
If Ping fails, return c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, map[string]string{"message": "service unavailable"})
Register route: e.GET('/readyz', readinessHandler.Handle)

checks: TS-01-6 passes: GET /healthz → 200 OK
TS-01-7 passes: GET /readyz with DB available → 200 OK
TS-01-E8 passes: GET /readyz with DB unavailable → 503 with {"message": "service unavailable"} and Content-Type: application/json
TS-01-P4 passes: property test for readiness reflecting database availability
01-PROP-4 invariant holds: 200 iff SELECT 1 succeeds
go vet ./internal/handler/... reports no issues

subtasks.title: Set up OS signal handling for SIGTERM and SIGINT
details: In cmd/server/main.go, create a channel to receive os.Signal notifications
Use signal.NotifyContext or signal.Notify to capture syscall.SIGTERM and syscall.SIGINT
Start the Echo HTTP server in a goroutine so main can wait for the signal channel

subtasks.title: Implement graceful shutdown with 30-second timeout
details: On signal receipt, call e.Shutdown(ctx) with a context that has a 30-second deadline: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
e.Shutdown stops the server from accepting new connections and waits for in-flight requests
After Shutdown returns (or timeout), close the database connection and exit cleanly
If Shutdown returns an error (timeout exceeded), log the error and exit with non-zero code

checks: TS-01-13 passes: SIGTERM during in-flight request → request completes with 201, new connections refused, process exits cleanly
TS-01-14 passes: SIGTERM with artificially slow request (60s) → process exits within ~32 seconds regardless
01-REQ-6.1 acceptance criteria met: no new connections accepted after signal
01-REQ-6.2 acceptance criteria met: force-exit after 30-second timeout

subtasks.title: Run full integration test suite and confirm all tests pass
details: Execute: go test ./internal/integration/... -v -count=1
All TS-01-1 through TS-01-18, TS-01-E1 through TS-01-E8 tests must pass
Record any failures and fix the implementation, not the tests

subtasks.title: Run property-based tests and confirm invariants hold
details: Execute property tests TS-01-P1 through TS-01-P5
Confirm 01-PROP-1 through 01-PROP-5 invariants all pass
Fix any implementation defects discovered

subtasks.title: Run linter and ensure zero warnings
details: Execute: go vet ./...
Execute: golangci-lint run (if configured) or staticcheck ./...
Resolve all reported issues before proceeding to wiring verification

checks: go test ./... -count=1 exits with code 0 — all tests pass
go vet ./... reports no issues
All seven requirement groups (01-REQ-1 through 01-REQ-7) have at least one passing test case
All five correctness properties (01-PROP-1 through 01-PROP-5) are validated by passing property tests

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification and End-to-End Smoke Tests

subtasks.title: Trace all execution paths end-to-end with no stubs remaining
details: Trace 01-PATH-1: confirm HTTP POST → BearerAuth middleware → EventsHandler.Handle → db.InsertEvent → c.NoContent(201) — each function actually calls the next, no TODOs or NotImplementedError stubs
Trace 01-PATH-2: confirm BearerAuth middleware returns echo.HTTPError(401) before EventsHandler is invoked
Trace 01-PATH-3: confirm EventsHandler payload validation returns echo.HTTPError(400) before db.InsertEvent is called
Trace 01-PATH-4: confirm GET /healthz → LivenessHandler returns 200 with no DB calls
Trace 01-PATH-5 and 01-PATH-6: confirm GET /readyz → ReadinessHandler → db.Ping → 200 or 503
Trace 01-PATH-7: confirm signal → e.Shutdown(ctx with 30s timeout) → DB close → process exit

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across all layers
details: Confirm EventsHandler checks the error returned by db.InsertEvent and returns 500 on non-nil error
Confirm ReadinessHandler checks the error returned by db.Ping and returns 503 on non-nil error
Confirm main() checks error returned by config.Load() and exits non-zero on error
Confirm main() checks error returned by db.Open() and exits non-zero on error
Confirm graceful shutdown logs and handles the error returned by e.Shutdown()

subtasks.title: Run all seven smoke tests with real components
details: Run TS-01-SMOKE-1: full end-to-end POST /v1/events → 201 + DB row verified
Run TS-01-SMOKE-2: missing Authorization → 401, no DB write
Run TS-01-SMOKE-3: valid token, missing Content-Type → 400, no DB write
Run TS-01-SMOKE-4: GET /healthz → 200
Run TS-01-SMOKE-5: GET /readyz with DB available → 200
Run TS-01-SMOKE-6: GET /readyz with DB unavailable → 503 {"message": "service unavailable"}
Run TS-01-SMOKE-7: SIGTERM with in-flight request → clean exit within 30s

subtasks.title: Stub and dead-code audit
details: Search codebase for 'TODO', 'FIXME', 'NotImplementedError', 'panic("not implemented")', bare 'return nil' in non-trivial paths, and empty function bodies
Execute: grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME\|panic("not implemented")' ./cmd ./internal
Confirm all handler methods contain real logic, not placeholder returns
Confirm db.InsertEvent, db.Ping, config.Load all contain real implementation, not stubs

subtasks.title: Cross-spec entry point and production wiring verification
details: Confirm that cmd/server/main.go (production entrypoint) calls config.Load, logger initialisation, db.Open, route registration, and e.Start in the correct order
Confirm that all routes (/v1/events, /healthz, /readyz) are registered on the Echo instance used by e.Start
Confirm that the BearerAuth middleware is applied exclusively to POST /v1/events and not to health check endpoints
Verify the Go module path github.com/agent-fox/example-service is used in all internal import paths
Confirm CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/server/... produces a working binary

checks: All seven smoke tests (TS-01-SMOKE-1 through TS-01-SMOKE-7) pass with real components, no mocks in the primary paths
grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME\|panic("not implemented")' ./cmd ./internal returns zero results
CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/server/... succeeds and produces a runnable binary
go test ./... -count=1 passes with all tests green after wiring is confirmed
Each execution path (01-PATH-1 through 01-PATH-7) is traceable in production code without any stub or placeholder functions in the call chain
All correctness properties (01-PROP-1 through 01-PROP-5) are validated by passing tests against the fully wired service
